body.title: CLOSED: watch_problems flaky tests — search_path isolation
body.result: fixed _conn() search_path isolation; unqualified _TABLE; batched lever_watcher UPDATEs; mocked escalate_to_channel in threshold tests — 12/12 pass, 0 regressions
body.description:
6 lever_watcher tests in test_watch_problems.py timeout (>30s) at every full-suite run. Two root causes: (1) watch_problems._conn() ignores IGOR_LOCAL_SEARCH_PATH so all module operations hit the live instance.watch_problems table instead of the test_instance_* schema created by pg_test_schema — the live table has many production rows, and lever_watcher processes them ALL; (2) lever_watcher opens a new psycopg2.connect() per problem in its update loop instead of one batched connection. Fix: set search_path in _conn() from IGOR_LOCAL_SEARCH_PATH; unqualify _TABLE to 'watch_problems'; simplify _MIGRATE_SQL to use ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS; batch lever_watcher updates into one connection; mock escalate_to_channel in test_lever_watcher_finds_match to prevent live channel writes.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/cognition/watch_problems.py, tests/test_watch_problems.py
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; test-plan-or-why-not
**Scope boundary:** In: _conn search_path, _TABLE unqualify, _MIGRATE_SQL simplify, lever_watcher single-conn, mock in test. Out: changing lever_watcher logic, adding new test cases, touching escalate.py.
**Test plan:** All 12 tests in tests/test_watch_problems.py pass in full suite without timeout.
